title: Delete the 9 bee_*.mjs shims from templates; retire registry helper metadata; rewrite pinned CLI tests to bee.mjs-only
action: Per D1: git rm the 9 bee_*.mjs shim files from skills/bee-hive/templates/. Per D5: remove the informational helper: field from every COMMAND_REGISTRY entry in lib/command-registry.mjs and drop the entry.helper assertion in test_bee_cli.mjs (~line 155). Manifest facts (verified): publicManifestEntries (~bee.mjs:1784) already omits helper via allowlist destructure — there is NO strip code to remove and the public manifest never carried helper; BUT computeManifestHash (~bee.mjs:1755) hashes the RAW registry including helper, so deleting the field CHANGES the drift hash — check for any pinned hash expectation (test or drift guard) and update it; the behavioral hash test at test_bee_cli.mjs:1022 needs no constant. Test rewrite in test_bee_cli.mjs — THREE MUTUALLY-DEPENDENT EDITS that must land together or the file throws ReferenceError: (1) delete the BEE_STATUS..BEE_FEEDBACK path constants (~43-51); (2) REWRITE (not just re-point) the DA5 verb-bijection guard: GROUP_HELPERS (~202-211) and helperRuntimeVerbs currently pass the magic probe arg __bee_bijection_probe__ (~line 222) to the shim scripts — that token is handled INSIDE the shims being deleted, bee.mjs does not know it; reconstruct verb enumeration from bee.mjs's own 'Unknown command ... Use: ...' contract lines / GROUP_USAGE_FALLBACKS (~bee.mjs:1489-1499+) by spawning bee.mjs with the group token and an unknown verb (verified: dispatcher prints the identical Use: line per group, including the reviews '(review modes: ...)' annotation); (3) delete ONLY parity-vs-shim checks — identified by callsites of runScript(BEE_*) — scattered at ~1052, 1059, 1090, 1098-1100, 1117, 1180, 1207 and running through ~1225, NOT a contiguous block. Lines ~1068 and ~1083 (demo-2 fixture create + list, runBee-only) MUST SURVIVE — the downstream dispatcher chain (~1104-1211) depends on the demo-2 cell they create. Rule: delete checks that call runScript(BEE_*); keep every runBee-only check. In test_lib.mjs the shim spawns are WHOLE SECTIONS, not single lines: cells CLI (~333, 425), bee_status (~1674), bee_feedback (~3312), bee_state CLI section (~3325-4080), bee_backlog add (~5658-5852), bee_reviews (~6226-6601), bee_status review (~6601-6830) — each spawn re-points to bee.mjs with the group token prepended; assertions unchanged. EXCEPTION per D3: test_lib.mjs:937 extractBashTargets('node bee_status.mjs --json 2>&1') is a legacy-shape PARSER INPUT for the write-guard transition path — leave it. In test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs keep legacy-name command-shape cases (LEGACY_HELPER_RE stays per D3); any case that EXECUTES a shim file switches to bee.mjs form. Also sweep templates/lib/*.mjs error-message hint strings that print shim names (e.g. guards.mjs:56-57 'bee_state.mjs set/gate/worker/scribing-run' / 'bee_backlog.mjs add', state.mjs/cells.mjs 'bee_cells.mjs verify') to `bee.mjs <group> <verb>` form — no TEMPLATES test asserts on these strings; hooks/test_write_guard.mjs DOES assert them but runs against the stale vendored .bee/bin/lib until the cell-6 self-onboard, so those hook-test assertion flips belong to cell 6, NOT here.

truths: no bee_*.mjs file remains under skills/bee-hive/templates/
bee.mjs --help --json manifest entries carry no helper field
the DA5 verb-bijection drift guard still runs and derives verbs from runtime output
every test that previously spawned a shim now exercises the same behavior through bee.mjs
prohibitions: no assertion deleted just because it is inconvenient — parity-with-shim assertions are the only legitimate deletions
do not touch scripts/onboard_bee.mjs, hooks/, or any prose docs in this cell

trace.outcome: Deleted the 9 retired bee_*.mjs shims from skills/bee-hive/templates/; removed the informational helper: field from every COMMAND_REGISTRY entry (D5) and its entry.helper test assertion; rewrote the DA5 verb-bijection guard to probe bee.mjs directly with the group token prepended instead of spawning the retired shims; rewrote every shim-spawning test in test_lib.mjs and test_bee_cli.mjs to call bee.mjs with its group token, deleting only the parity-vs-shim checks that compared bee.mjs output against a spawned shim; swept error-message hint strings in lib/state.mjs, lib/cells.mjs, lib/guards.mjs, lib/reviews.mjs, lib/inject.mjs from bee_*.mjs form to bee.mjs <group> <verb> form; synced .bee/bin/lib/* to match (this repo self-hosts, so the standing template<->vendor byte-parity guard requires it); fixed a pre-existing unrelated gap in test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs's VENDORED_LIB_MODULES (missing claims.mjs/backlog.mjs) discovered while running this cell's verify chain. Full verify chain green: test_lib.mjs 292/0, test_bee_cli.mjs 116/0, test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs 21/0, no bee_*.mjs remain.
deviations: Auto-fixed a pre-existing bug in test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs's VENDORED_LIB_MODULES (missing claims.mjs and backlog.mjs), confirmed via git stash to predate this cell; without it, this cell's own verify command could never pass since the file is chained in the cell's verify.

Deleted 7 parity-vs-shim checks in test_bee_cli.mjs whose entire purpose was diffing bee.mjs output against a spawned bee_*.mjs shim (now impossible since the shim is deleted); replaced the frozen-key-refusal and cells-ready assertions with equivalent non-parity checks so the underlying behavior stays covered, per the cell's own instruction to delete only parity-with-shim checks.

Synced .bee/bin/lib/{command-registry,state,cells,guards,reviews,inject}.mjs to their edited templates siblings — required for the pre-existing standing vendor-parity guard test in test_lib.mjs to pass in this self-hosting repo; did not touch .bee/bin/bee_*.mjs shims (D2 remove_helper pass is cell sr-2's job) or scripts/onboard_bee.mjs.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": [
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs"
  ],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [
    "test_bee_cli.mjs: removed the 9 BEE_STATUS..BEE_FEEDBACK shim-path constants and the now-dead runScript() helper; removed the entry.helper manifest assertion (D5); rewrote the DA5 bijection check (GROUP_HELPERS/helperRuntimeVerbs) to probe bee.mjs directly with each group token prepended instead of spawning the retired shim files; deleted 7 parity-vs-shim checks that diffed bee.mjs output against a runScript(BEE_*) spawn (status --json/text, cells ready --json/text, cells update frozen-key, reservations list --active-only, decisions active), replacing the frozen-key and cells-ready checks with equivalent non-parity assertions so the underlying behavior stays covered",
    "test_lib.mjs: repointed cells add/update CLI checks, the bee_feedback.mjs rank CLI check, the bee_state.mjs CLI section's runBeeState() helper (used by ~40 checks), the bee_backlog.mjs add section's runBeeBacklog() helper, the bee_reviews.mjs CLI section's runBeeReviews() helper, and the bee_status.mjs review-integration section's runBeeStatus() helper — each now spawns bee.mjs with its group token prepended instead of the deleted shim file; updated 2 hint-text assertions (buildSessionPreamble, cap-refusal FIX text) to match the new bee.mjs-form hint strings",
    "test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs: added 'claims.mjs' and 'backlog.mjs' to VENDORED_LIB_MODULES (pre-existing baseline gap unrelated to shim-retire, found while running this cell's verify chain — state.mjs imports claims.mjs and cells.mjs imports backlog.mjs, both missing from this fixture's transitive-import vendoring list, which made the hook's dynamic import throw and fail every guard open; confirmed pre-existing via `git stash` against pristine HEAD before touching it)"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Reproduced directly: with the working tree stashed back to pre-cell HEAD, deleting only bee_status.mjs and bee_cells.mjs (this cell's D1 action) while keeping the original test files causes test_bee_cli.mjs to regress from 121 passed/0 failed to 115 passed/6 failed, all via spawnSync ENOENT/status-null failures on the deleted shim paths (byte-identity parity checks that spawned bee_status.mjs/bee_cells.mjs directly). Separately, test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs was 12 passed/9 failed at the literal pre-cell HEAD (git stash-verified) due to the pre-existing missing claims.mjs/backlog.mjs vendoring gap — fixed in passing since the file is in this cell's scope.",
  "verification_run": "node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs && [ -z \"$(ls skills/bee-hive/templates/bee_*.mjs 2>/dev/null)\" ] -> exit 0; test_lib.mjs 292 passed/0 failed, test_bee_cli.mjs 116 passed/0 failed, test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs 21 passed/0 failed, no bee_*.mjs files remain under skills/bee-hive/templates/. Also confirmed no collateral damage: skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs still 0 failures (repo's configured baseline verify), and .bee/bin/lib/{command-registry,state,cells,guards,reviews,inject}.mjs synced byte-identical to their templates siblings so the standing vendor-parity guard (test_lib.mjs) passes in this self-hosting repo.",
  "deliberate_exceptions": [
    "hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs itself was not modified (explicit cell prohibition) even though it contains the resolveCliCommandName/checkCliShape logic exercised by test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs — only the test fixture's VENDORED_LIB_MODULES list (a templates/tests file in this cell's scope) was corrected",
    "scripts/onboard_bee.mjs (D2 remove_helper pass) and the AGENTS.md/skills prose sweep (D4) are out of scope for this cell by design — later cells sr-2 through sr-6 per plan.md"
  ]
}
trace.verify_output: test_lib.mjs: 292 passed, 0 failed; test_bee_cli.mjs: 116 passed, 0 failed; test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs: 21 passed, 0 failed; ls check: no bee_*.mjs files remain under skills/bee-hive/templates/. Combined chain exit code 0.
